Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Petitioners First Set Of Special Interrogatories, Supporting Declaration Of Abenicio Cisneros And Memorandum In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Jun-26-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Abencio J. Cisneros, Esq., Law Offices of Abencio J. Cisneros, 707-653-0438, appearing for Petitioner Michael Johnson; (CourtCall)
Jennifer C. Addams Esq., Carolyn Tsai,Esq., California Department of justice, 415-510-3363, appearing for Defendant California Department of Managed Healthcare;(CourtCall)
No other appearance.

All parties present stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Katherine Gallo hearing today's motion.

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER MICHAEL JOHNSON Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Petitioners First Set Of Special Interrogatories,

The motion is Denied on procedural grounds. The motion was filed prior to moving party serving their responses which was due 30 days after June 1, 2020. See the Presiding Judge's April 30, 2020 Order re: Implementation of Emergency Relief. There is no meaningful meet and confer after the responses were served as required by CCP 2030.300(3)(b)(1). See Obregon v. Superior Court (1998) 67 CA4th 424, 431. The separate statement of items in dispute fails to have the responding party's responses as required by CRC, Rule 3.1345(c) and fails to provide any substantive factual or legal reasons as required by CRC, Rule 3.1345(c)(3). The court also notes that the requested information is better suited by serving a Request for Production of Documents. Counsel for Respondent to prepare and submit a proposed order which is verbatim to the courts tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and approved as to form to Judge Pro Tem Katherine Gallo at klgallo@discoveryreferee.com. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
